# Copilot instructions for pyA2L

## Project overview
- Purpose: Parse ASAP2/A2L files, persist them as SQLite `.a2ldb` databases, inspect/validate them with SQLAlchemy models, and optionally export back to A2L.
- Package name on PyPI is **`pya2ldb`**; import namespace is `pya2l`. Default import encoding is latin-1; `.a2ldb` extension is implied by helpers.

## Architecture

### Core data flow
```
.a2l file
  → A2LParser (pya2l/a2lparser.py) + native ext (a2lparser_ext via pybind11/CMake)
  → pya2l.model SQLAlchemy ORM (SQLite .a2ldb)
  → SessionProxy (pya2l/model/__init__.py) — thin wrapper adding parse_ifdata / setup_ifdata_parser
  → pya2l.api.inspect  (read-only helpers: Measurement, Characteristic, CompuMethod, …)
  → pya2l.api.create   (mutation creators: MeasurementCreator, CharacteristicCreator, …)
  → pya2l.api.validate (Validator — yields Message namedtuples)
  → optional export: pya2l/__init__.py _render_a2l (Mako template pya2l/cgen/templates/a2l.tmpl)
                  or pya2l/imex/a2l_exporter.py (standalone, SQLAlchemy selectinload approach)
```

### Entry-points (pya2l/__init__.py)
| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `import_a2l(file_name, ...)` | Parse .a2l → write .a2ldb, return SessionProxy |
| `open_existing(file_name)` | Open existing .a2ldb, auto-recover if corrupt |
| `open_create(file_name)` | Open if exists, else import |
| `export_a2l(db_name, output)` | Render .a2ldb back to A2L text |
| `DB` class | Static wrapper around the four functions above |

### Model layer (pya2l/model/__init__.py)
- All ORM classes inherit from `Base` (SQLAlchemy declarative). Table name is auto-derived from class name (lowercase).
- Primary key is always `rid` (integer). Every model class carries two class-level tuples:
  - `__required_parameters__` — `Parameter(name, type_, multiple)` instances
  - `__optional_elements__` — `Element(name, keyword_name, multiple)` instances
- Column factory helpers (`StdFloat`, `StdShort`, `StdUShort`, `StdLong`, `StdULong`, `StdString`, `StdIdent`) keep column definitions uniform.
- `CompareByPositionMixIn` (pya2l/model/mixins.py): add to identifier-list models that need positional sorting.
- SQLite is opened with WAL mode, `FOREIGN_KEYS=ON`, `SYNCHRONOUS=OFF`, busy_timeout=5 s — set via `@event.listens_for(Engine, "connect")`.
- `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 10` — bump this when the schema changes and add migration logic.

### API layer (pya2l/api/)
- `inspect.py` — read-only wrapper classes (e.g. `Measurement`, `Characteristic`, `CompuMethod`). Use `cached_property` for expensive lookups. `FilteredList` provides lazy filtered views.
- `create.py` — creator classes (e.g. `MeasurementCreator`). Each creator takes a session, calls `session.add(obj)`, and exposes a `commit()` / `rollback()` pair.
- `validate.py` — `Validator` yields `Message = namedtuple("Message", "type category diag_code text")` with `Level` / `Category` IntEnums.

### Computation functions (pya2l/functions.py)
Implements compumethod evaluation: `Identical`, `Linear`, `RatFunc`, `Coeffs`/`CoeffsLinear` dataclasses, `LookupTable`, `LookupTableWithRanges`, `InterpolatedTable`, `Formula` (via numexpr). Used by `inspect.py` to compute physical values.

### AML / IF_DATA (pya2l/aml/)
`setup_ifdata_parser` on `SessionProxy` initialises `IfDataParser` (in `pya2l/aml/ifdata_parser.py`). Call `session.parse_ifdata(sections)` to decode raw IF_DATA blobs. Do not hand-parse IF_DATA — use these helpers.

### Native extensions
`a2lparser_ext`, `amlparser_ext`, `preprocessor` are pybind11-based C++ extensions built by CMake (see `CMakeLists.txt`, `build_ext.py`). Headers live in `pya2l/extensions/`.

### Generated / read-only files
`pya2l/aml_parser.cpp`, `pya2l/aml_parser.hpp` etc. under `pya2l/extensions/` are C++ sources for the native extensions — regenerated by CMake/pybind11. Do not edit manually.

## Build / install
- Dev install (PEP 517 backend, builds native extensions): `pip install -v -e .` (requires Python 3.10+, CMake ≥ 3.12, C/C++ toolchain, pybind11).
- Poetry workflow (matches CI): `poetry install --with dev` (use `poetry config virtualenvs.create false` to install into current env if desired).
- Source/wheel artifacts: `python -m build`.
- Docs: `python -m pip install -r docs/requirements.txt sphinx` then `sphinx-build -b html docs docs/_build/html` (entrypoint `docs/index.rst` / `docs/README.rst`).
- Versioning: `poetry run bumpver update --patch` (or `--minor` / `--major`); updates `pyproject.toml` and `pya2l/version.py`.
- Python 3.10+ required (`python = "^3.10"` in pyproject.toml).

## Test commands
- Full suite: `poetry run pytest` (tests live in `pya2l/tests`, pytest settings in `pyproject.toml`).
- Single test/module: `python -m pytest pya2l/tests/test_a2l_parser.py -k basic -q`
- Tests parse inline A2L strings via `A2LParser().parse(str(a2l_file), in_memory=True)` — the `db` fixture in `test_api_inspect.py` shows the canonical pattern.
- For `pya2l.api.create` tests, build a bare in-memory session directly: `sqlalchemy.create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:")` + `model.Base.metadata.create_all(engine)`.

## Linting/format/security
- Format/check: `poetry run black --check .` (line length 132), `poetry run isort --check-only .`.
- Lint: `poetry run ruff check .` (target py312), `poetry run flake8`.
- Security: `poetry run bandit -c bandit.yml -r pya2l`.
- Pre-commit (recommended locally): `poetry run pre-commit run --all-files`.
- Generated parser files and build outputs are excluded from lint/format (see tool configs in `pyproject.toml`); avoid manual edits there.

## Key conventions
- Prefer high-level APIs over direct SQLAlchemy model manipulation: `pya2l.import_a2l`/`open_existing` → `pya2l.api.inspect` (reads) → `pya2l.api.create` (writes) → `pya2l.api.validate.Validator` (checks).
- CLI: `poetry run a2ldb-imex -i file.a2l` to import (add `-L` to place `.a2ldb` in CWD); `poetry run a2ldb-imex -e file.a2ldb -o out.a2l` to export; `-V` for version.
- When handling IF_DATA/AML, use `pya2l.aml.ifdata_parser` and `session.setup_ifdata_parser` / `session.parse_ifdata` — do not write bespoke IF_DATA parsing.
- `examples/` contains a large set of real-world `.a2l` and `.aml` files useful for manual testing and benchmarking.
- Inline A2L strings in tests follow the pattern: `ASAP2_VERSION 1 71 /begin PROJECT … /begin MODULE … /end MODULE /end PROJECT`.
- `pya2l/cgen/templates/a2l.tmpl` is a Mako template used by `_render_a2l` for A2L export; edit it when adding new keyword export support.
